I read the prompt and prior log as an external research record. The prompt fixes the special submission format: first generators sigma,tau, implicit tame relation tau^sigma=tau^2, later generators in the pro-2 core, and exponents represented modulo M=85667662080.The prior log leaves one serious unrefuted candidate, while earlier scalar, plain-ŌÄ2, and fixed-ŌĄ‚ąí2 variants have recorded finite-quotient failures.Jarden‚ÄďShusterman also record the relevant external boundary: local absolute Galois groups are finitely presented, Diekert‚Äôs dyadic result does not cover K=Q2, and the minimal generator/relation counts are [K:Qp]+2 and [K:Qp]+1.Tel Aviv University - Mathematics

The surviving candidate is still variables sigma,tau,x,y definitions nx = (x*tau^50932630783)^40491355905 ny = (y*tau)^40491355905 relations x*sigma^45176306175*x*sigma^40491355905*x^-1*nx*ny^sigma*y^-1

Equivalently, with ŌÄ=ŌÄ2‚ąąZ and ő∑=2ŌÄ on odd tame inertia,r=xŌÉ‚ąíŌÄxŌÉŌÄx‚ąí1(xŌĄ‚ąíő∑)ŌÄ ((yŌĄ)ŌÄ)ŌÉy‚ąí1,where ŌÄ‚Č°40491355905,‚ąíŌÄ‚Č°45176306175,‚ąíő∑‚Č°50932630783(modM).The 2-primary coordinate of ‚ąíő∑ is irrelevant here, because this exponent is applied only to the tame inertia generator ŌĄ, whose finite tame images have odd order.

1. Two formulas that should be frozen in any future write-up Several prior turns oscillate between reciprocal formulas. The safest way to state them is by the equations they solve.

Sign-cover norm coefficient Let V=kerőļ be the sign double cover, let t be its deck involution, and work on a nontrivial odd tame-inertia character block ŌĄ‚Ü¶őĪÓÄ†=1. ForNa (u)=(uŌĄa)ŌÄ,the Fox coefficient isDa=j=0‚ąĎŌÄ‚ąí1(tőĪa)j.

It satisfies (1‚ąítőĪa)Da=1‚ąí(tőĪa)ŌÄ.Since ŌÄ‚Č°1(mod2) on t, and ŌÄ‚Č°0(mode) on an e-th odd tame character,(tőĪa)ŌÄ=t.

Therefore the correct formula isDa=(1‚ąít)(1‚ąítőĪa)‚ąí1.This is the direction that makes the unit/radical argument work: 1‚ąítőĪa is a 2-adic unit for őĪaÓÄ†=1, while 1‚ąít is radical. Hence Da is radical, and the coefficient of the correction factorx‚ąí1(xŌĄ‚ąíő∑)ŌÄ is ‚ąí1+D‚ąíő∑, a unit. Thus the nontrivial odd tame-character blocks contribute only unit Smith pivots.

Trivial tame-inertia block Fix the convention that T is the ŌÉ-cycle operator and s‚Č°ŌÄ(modf). The mod-2 linear relation in the ŌĄ-invariant block is(1+T‚ąís)X+(1+T)Y=0.

Write f=2a m, m odd. Then s‚Č°1(mod2a),s‚Č°0(modm).

If f is even, s is odd, so 1+T‚ą£1+T‚ąís. The valid elimination is the polynomial Ps (T) satisfying (1+T)Ps (T)=1+T‚ąís,that is,Ps(T)=1+T1+T‚ąís,up to the harmless convention T‚ÜĒT‚ąí1. The reciprocal is not legitimate in F2 [T]/(Tf‚ąí1).With h(T)=1+Ps (T)+Ps(T‚ąí1),for a nontrivial f-th root ő∂,(1+ő∂)h(ő∂)=ő∂‚ąís+ő∂s+1=ő∂‚ąís(1+ő∂2s+1).But 2s+1 is odd and congruent to 1(modm), so gcd(f,2s+1)=1. Hence h has no nontrivial f-th-root zero, and h(1)=1. The trivial block nonsingularity argument is sound with this convention.With these two formulas fixed, the prior finite-tame abelianization checks remain internally consistent:Ue,fab‚ČÉZ2ef+1‚äēC2,and, for the sign kernel,(kerőļ)ab‚ČÉZ22ef+1‚äēC4.These are strong consistency checks, but not a proof of the full profinite presentation.2. New refinement: relation-preserving corrections for the ‚Äúuniversal square‚ÄĚ directions The prior turns split the first marked IA ambiguity, after odd-Hall rigidity, into two universal square directions and the genuinely mixed involution-indexed commutator directions. I agree with that split, but one more layer must be added: not every degree-two IA direction should be treated as a filtered-action direction unless it preserves the DemuŇ°kin relation one layer further.Use the reduced degree-one model for a finite tame quotient őĒ=őĒe,f:L1‚ČÉF2 [őĒ]‚äēF2 b‚äēF2S,with marked S=ŌÉf, and quadratic initial relationq=g‚ąąőĒ‚ąĎXg[2]+[b,S].

A degree-one IA derivation őī:L1‚ÜíL2 preserves the relation to the next layer exactly when őī(q)=g‚ąąőĒ‚ąĎ[Xg,őī(Xg)]+[őī(b),S]+[b,őī(S)]=0 in L3. The marked condition is őī(S)=0.

The S[2]-direction is harmless:őīS (b)=S[2],őīS(Xg)=0,őīS (S)=0,becauseőīS (q)=[S[2],S]=0.

The b[2]-direction is subtler. The raw derivation őī(b)=b[2], őī(Xg)=0, őī(S)=0 gives őī(q)=[b[2],S],which is not formally zero. However it has a canonical correction:őīb (b)=b[2],őīb(Xg)=[Xg,b]¬†for¬†every¬†g‚ąąőĒ,őīb(S)=0.

Indeed,g‚ąĎ[Xg,őīb(Xg)]=g‚ąĎ[Xg,[Xg,b]]=g‚ąĎ[Xg[2],b].

Using the relation ‚ąĎgXg[2]=[b,S], this becomes [[b,S],b].

In characteristic 2, Jacobi/restricted-Lie identities give [[b,S],b]=[b[2],S].

Thereforeőīb (q)=[b[2],S]+[b[2],S]=0.So the two universal square directions are not merely ‚Äúabelian-visible‚ÄĚ; they also have explicit relation-preserving lifts. This matters because an arithmetic comparison through L√ó/(L√ó)4 can see the two square coefficients, but a proof of the filtered operator action must also match the associated nonabelian correction, especially the correction őīb(Xg)=[Xg,b].

3. Mixed involution directions and the remaining target

For f even, write f=2a m, m odd, and let

H=‚ü®ŌĄňČ,ŌÉňČ2a‚ü©,P=őĒe,f/H‚ČÉC2a.

The first marked IA ambiguity reduces toAe,f‚ČÉH1(P,L2H).

The nonfree part is(L2H)nonfree‚ČÉJ1‚äē2‚äēJ2 a‚ąí1‚äēI(e,f),I(e,f)=gcd(e,2f/2+1),where Jr is the length-r unipotent indecomposable F2[C2a]-module.

The two J1 ‚Äôs are b[2]and S[2]. The other summands are indexed by involutionsőīj=ŌĄňČjŌÉňČf/2,j(1+2f/2)‚Č°0(mode).

For such an involution, setZj=h‚ąąH‚ąĎ[Xh,Xhőīj].The relation-preserving correction for this direction is the expected generalization of the S3 case:őīj (b)=Zj,őīj(Xh)=[Xhőīj,S],őīj(Xhőīj)=[Xh,S],őīj (S)=0,with zero on the remaining displayed representatives. Thenőīj (q)=0 by Jacobi in characteristic 2. Moreover the same restricted-power tower persists:őīj,n (b)=Zj[2n],with the usual correctionTj,n=(adZj)2n‚ąí1 (S),őīj,n(Xh)=[Xhőīj,Tj,n],őīj,n(Xhőīj)=[Xh,Tj,n].

Thus the remaining gap is not just the first Zj -layer; the involution-indexed directions have higher restricted-power descendants.

There is also a Frobenius-lift gauge: replacingŌÉňČbyŌĄňČkŌÉňČ sends j‚üľj+k(2f/2‚ąí1)(mode).So in the (e,f)=(5,4) test, the five involution coordinates are labeled only after fixing a specific tame Frobenius lift. Without that convention, one should compare the orbit of the five Zj -directions, not a named Z0.

4. What I think is actually proved versus still hand-waved The strongest surviving candidate is not refuted by the recorded finite tests. The following pieces look genuinely established, modulo the caveat that they are computations in the proposed presentation rather than an isomorphism proof:Ue,fab‚ČÉZ2ef+1‚äēC2,(kerőļ)ab‚ČÉZ22ef+1‚äēC4,the nontrivial odd tame-character blocks have unit Smith pivots, and the finite-tame cup-product blocks are consistent with the DemuŇ°kin target. The need for operator data is not cosmetic: DemuŇ°kin-with-operators arguments explicitly track invariant/cohomological operator structure, not merely the abstract kernel.arXiv What is not proved is the decisive dyadic formation step. Matching abelianization, cup product, sign kernel, and cyclotomic branches does not determine the full filtered P-operator action. The next sharp test is no longer ‚Äúcompute another Smith form.‚ÄĚ It is:For (e,f)=(5,4), fix a concrete tame degree-20 extension and a Frobenius-lift convention. First match the two square coordinates b[2], S[2]through the equivariant action on L√ó/(L√ó)4. Then match the five involution-indexed nonabelian coordinates Zj through GL (2)/GL(2)3 or GL (2)/GL(2)5, in the same gauge.

ESTABLISHED: The live dynamic candidate remains the only unrefuted candidate. The stable formulas areDa=(1‚ąít)(1‚ąítőĪa)‚ąí1,(1+T)Ps (T)=1+T‚ąís.I added explicit relation-preserving corrections for the two universal square directions, especiallyőīb (b)=b[2],őīb(Xg)=[Xg,b],őīb (S)=0,and verified őīb (q)=0. The involution-indexed Zj -directions and their restricted-power towers remain the genuinely nonabelian mixed-tame targets.

GAP: This is still not a proof of the presentation of GQ2. The missing ingredient is a dyadic formation/rigidity theorem, or an explicit arithmetic comparison, showing that the candidate‚Äôs exact filtered tame action agrees with the actual arithmetic Frobenius/tame-inertia action over every finite tame subextension.

NEXT: For (e,f)=(5,4), compute the candidate‚Äôs seven first-IA coordinates in a fixed Frobenius gauge: two square coordinates plus the five involution-indexed Zj -coordinates. Then compare those with explicit Kummer/Witt arithmetic for the corresponding tame degree-20 extension.[BREAKTHROUGH]
